source_metadata.title_raw: Jesse Mainardi - I-19-197 - March 13, 2020 - San Francisco

content.full_text:
Jesse Mainardi 
Mainardi Law 
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
March 13, 2020 
315 Montgomery St., 9th Floor 
San Francisco, CA 94104 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-19-197 
Dear Mr. Mainardi: 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the 
Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. Because your inquiry is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for 
informal assistance. 2 
QUESTION 
What are the identification and disclaimer requirements for electronic slate mailers 
distributed via social media post, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial websites, and 
Google search result ads? 
CONCLUSION 
All slate mailers distributed by a slate mailer organization, including those distributed 
electronically, must include sender identification information on the "outside" of the slate mailer 
and both sender identification, a "Notice to Voters" and asterisks on the slate mailer itself as 
described in further detail below. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or fonnal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
FileNo. 1-19-197 
Page No. 2 
Your firm represents the Edwin M. Lee Democratic Club Voter Guide (the "Voter Guide"), 
a slate mailer organization based in the city ofNovato. The Voter Guide intends to support a 
number of San Francisco candidates and measures in connection with the March 3, 2020 primary 
election, including candidates running for the Democratic County Central Committee ("DCCC") 
and an emergency bond measure. The Voter Guide expects to receive payment from certain 
candidates who wish to appear on the slate mailer, as well as from third parties wishing to support 
certain candidates and measures. In addition to producing slate mailers for distribution via 
traditional mail (postal service), the Voter Guide intends to produce a variety of electronic slate 
mailers, to be distributed via social media, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial 
websites, and Google search result ads. 
The first type of slate mailer would be distributed electronically via social media post, e-
mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial websites, or Google search result ads. The slate 
mailer would be preceded by an image featuring either the Voter Guide's logo, an image of late 
Mayer Ed Lee, or an image of one or more of the supported candidates and measure on the slate 
mailer. Along with the image would be an exhortation to click through to see the full slate mailer. 
Clicking on the image would take the user to a webpage with the full Voter Guide slate mailer as 
well as a downloadable PDF copy of the slate mailer for use on election day. In the alternative, the 
slate mailer may be distributed via email that will contain a link to the full slate mailer or an 
attached PDF of the full slate mailer. 
The second type of slate mailer distributed via social media or commercial website post 
would be in the form of a video (instead of an image that when clicked leads to the full slate 
mailer), in which the video consists ofa sequence of images of some or all of the Voter Guide's 
endorsed or opposed candidates or measures, with the following appearing directly below the 
photographs: (1) names of those depicted, (2) the offices for which the depicted candidates are 
running, (3) asterisks, as appropriate, and (4) the Voter Guide's positions on the 
candidates/measures ( e.g., in favor or against). These photographs would be preceded or succeeded 
by an image that displays the name and address of the Voter Guide as well as the "Notice to Voter" 
disclaimer. There would also be a display, at the beginning or end, of an image associated with the 
Voter Guide (such as its logo). Some versions may also include a feature where if the video is un-
muted (automatically or by the user), there would be a spoken statement with: (1) the candidate or 
measure names; (2) the offices for which they are running; and (3) the Voter Guide's positions on 
the candidates/measures. These statements would be spoken concurrently with the images of the 
candidates or measures. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act defines a slate mailer as a mass mailing (more than 200 substantially similar pieces 
of mail sent in a calendar month) that supports or opposes a total of four or more candidates or 
ballot measures. (Section 82048.3.) A slate mailer organization, meanwhile, is any person who 
directly or indirectly: 


FileNo. I-19-197 
Page No. 3 
• 
Is involved in the production of one or more slate mailers and exercises control over the 
selection of candidates and measures to be supported or opposed in the slate mailers; and 
• 
Receives or is promised payments totaling $500 or more in a calendar year for the 
production of one or more slate mailers. 
(Section 82048.4) 
The sender identification provisions under Section 84305.5(a)(l) of the Act require a slate 
mailer organization to include its name, street address, and the city of the organization on the 
outside of each piece of slate mail and on at least one insert of each piece of slate mail it sends out. 3 
In addition, the Act requires a slate mailer organization or committee to designate by an asterisk 
each candidate and each ballot measure supported or opposed in the mailer for which payment of 
$100 or more has been received by the organization or committee ( either from the candidate, ballot 
measure committee, or from any other person "at the behest" of a candidate or ballot measure 
committee). (Section 84305.5 and Regulation 18435.5(a).) 
The slate mailer disclaimer specifically prescribed by the Act in Section 84305.5(a)(2) 
consists of the following statement: 
"NOTICE TO VOTERS THIS DOCUMENT WAS PREPARED BY (name of slate 
mailer organization or committee primarily formed to support or oppose one or more 
ballot measures), NOT AN OFFICIAL POLITICAL PARTY ORGANIZATION. 
Appearance in this mailer does not necessarily imply endorsement of others 
appearing in this mailer, nor does it imply endorsement of, or opposition to, any 
issues set forth in this mailer. Appearance is paid for and authorized by each 
candidate and ballot measure which is designated by an* ."4 
This disclaimer must appear in 8-point Roman type, with a reasonable degree of color 
contrast on a plain background. (Section 84305.5 and Regulation 18435.5.) Each candidate and 
each ballot measure that has paid to appear on the slate mailer must be designated with an asterisk 
immediately following the name of the candidate or ballot measure. (Section 84305.5(a)(4).) 
As specified in Regulation 18435.S(d),5 the slate mailer identification and disclaimer 
requirements also apply to slate mailers distributed electronically. However, neither the Act nor 
Commission Regulations specify the manner in which the identifications and disclaimers must 
appear for electronic slate mailers in various formats. For this reason, we must examine the current 
identification and disclaimer requirements and determine how to best apply them to electronic slate 
mailers as well. 
3 A post office box may be used in lieu of a street address if the street address of the slate mailer organization 
or the committee primarily formed to support or oppose one or more ballot measures is a matter of public record with 
the Secretary of State. (Section 84305.5(a)(l).) 
4 As we advised in the Woocher Advice Letter, No. A-15-004, an electronic slate mailer it is permissible to 
modify the disclaimer required by Section 84305.5 slightly to make it fit the digital environment rather than postal 
mailing, changing the words "document" and "mailer" to "ad." 
5 Regulation I 8435.5(d) provides: "In addition to applying to slate mailers sent by traditional mail, the slate 
mailer identification and disclaimer requirements of Section 84305.5 apply to slate mailers distributed electronically." 


FileNo. I-19-197 
Page No. 4 
Electronic Slate Mailers Distributed Online or Via Email or Text Message Featuring a 
Clickable Image or Link that Leads to the Full Slate Mailer 
You have asked what identification and disclaimer requirements are required for electronic 
slate mailers distributed via social media post, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial 
websites, or Google search result ads. These slate mailers would begin with a clickable image or 
link that leads to the full slate mailer. Alternatively, an email may provide the full slate mailer as an 
attached PDF. 
Under Section 84305.5, the name, street address and city of the slate mailer organization 
must be "shown on the outside of each piece of slate mail and on at least one the inserts included 
with each piece of slate mail." In the case of an electronic slate mailer featuring an image leading to 
the full slate mailer the image may be considered the "outside" of the slate mailer and for an email 
or text message containing a link or attachment to the full slate mailer the body of the email or text 
message may be considered akin to the "outside" of the attached piece of slate mail described in 
Section 84305.5. 
Electronic Slate Mailers on Social Media and Other Websites: The slate mailer sender 
identification and disclaimer requirements may be satisfied by treating the clickable image as the 
"outside" of the slate mailer. Appearing along with the clickable image should be the name, street 
address, and city of the slate mailer organization. The full slate mailer that the clickable image leads 
to must contain the name, street address and city of the slate mailer organization as well as the 
required Notice to Voters statement. Moreover, each candidate or ballot measure identified that has 
paid to appear in the slate mailer must be immediately followed by the required asterisk. 
Electronic Slate Mailers Distributed Via Email: The slate mailer sender identification and 
disclaimer requirements may be satisfied by treating the body of the email as the "outside" of the 
attached or linked piece of slate mail. The body of the email itself must contain the name, street 
address and city of the slate mailer organization. The linked or attached PDF copy of the slate 
mailer must contain the name, street address and city of the slate mailer organization as well as the 
required Notice to Voters statement and each candidate or ballot measure identified that has paid to 
appear in the slate mailer must be immediately followed by the required asterisk. 
Electronic Slate Mailers Distributed Via Text Message: Similarly, the slate mailer sender 
identification and disclaimer requirements may be satisfied by treating the body of the text message 
as the "outside" of the linked slate mailer. The body of the text message should contain the name, 
street address and city of the slate mailer organization and the slate mailer to which the text 
message links must contain the name, street address and city of the slate mailer organization as well 
as the required Notice to Voters statement and required asterisks identifying each candidate or 
ballot measure that has paid to appear in the slate mailer. 6 
6 While we specifically address electronic communications that provide an attached slate mailer or link to the 
slate mailer, we note that nothing precludes an initial electronic communication from providing the entirely of the slate 
mailer. For example, an emailed slate mailer may provide the full slate mailer with proper disclosures in the body of the 
email as opposed to providing an attached slate mailer or a link to the slate mailer. 


Electronic Slate Mailers featuring a Video or Series of Static Images 
File No. I-19-197 
Page No. 5 
You have also inquired about the required identification and disclaimers for electronic slate 
mailers featuring videos, or a sequence of static images. Some of these would be distributed via 
social media and would be accompanied by a spoken statement with: (1) the candidate or measure 
names; (2) the offices for which they are running; and (3) the Voter Guide's positions on the 
candidates/measures. These statements would be spoken concurrently with the images of the 
candidates or measures when unmuted either automatically or by the user. 
The facts of this scenario are substantially similar to those addressed in the Woocher Advice 
Letter, No. A-15-004. In the Woocher letter, we advised that electronic slate mailers in the form of 
"pop-up" ads on websites complied with the requirements of Section 84305.5 by including the 
required identification and disclaimers in a frame ( or frames) preceding or following each ad-in 
that case, a sequence of frames featuring the candidates' photos and the offices for which they were 
running. 
Therefore, an electronic slate mailer featuring a video or sequence of static images-
whether distributed via social media post, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial 
websites, or Google search result ads-may comply with the Act's slate mailer sender identification 
and disclaimer requirements by including the name, street address and city of the slate mailer 
organization and "Notice to Voters" disclaimer in a frame (or frames) preceding or following the 
video or sequence of images. Additionally, each candidate or ballot measure that has paid to appear 
on the slate mailer must be identified with the required asterisk. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5746. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
Toren Lewis 
Counsel, Legal Division 
TAL:sal

sections.question:
What are the identification and disclaimer requirements for electronic slate mailers 
distributed via social media post, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial websites, and 
Google search result ads?

All slate mailers distributed by a slate mailer organization, including those distributed 
electronically, must include sender identification information on the "outside" of the slate mailer 
and both sender identification, a "Notice to Voters" and asterisks on the slate mailer itself as 
described in further detail below. 
1 The Political Refonn Act is contained in2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or fonnal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

distributed via social media post, e-mail, text message, "banner ads" on commercial websites, and
